graph_title: IEC 60825-1 Standards and MPE Limits Knowledge Graph

standards.name: IEC 60825-1:2014
standards.title: Safety of laser products - Part 1: Equipment classification and requirements

standards.name: ANSI Z136.1-2000
standards.title: American National Standard for Safe Use of Lasers

relationships.description: Defines MPE tables and correction factors

laser_classes.name: Class 1
laser_classes.description: Safe under reasonably foreseeable conditions of operation

laser_classes.name: Class 1M
laser_classes.description: Safe except when viewing with optical instruments

laser_classes.name: Class 2
laser_classes.description: Safe due to aversion response (visible only)

laser_classes.name: Class 2M
laser_classes.description: Class 2 except when viewing with optical instruments

laser_classes.name: Class 3R
laser_classes.description: Low risk, normally safe with limited beam viewing

laser_classes.name: Class 3B
laser_classes.description: Direct beam viewing hazard, normally safe for diffuse reflections

laser_classes.name: Class 4
laser_classes.description: High power, all viewing hazardous, fire and diffuse reflection hazard

laser_classes.name: Class 1C
laser_classes.description: Contact medical application, closed beam path

limits.name: Accessible Emission Limit
limits.description: Maximum accessible emission level permitted within a particular class

relationships.description: AE compared against AEL for classification

limits.name: Maximum Permissible Exposure
limits.description: Level of laser radiation to which a person may be exposed without hazardous effect

relationships.description: MPE used to calculate Nominal Ocular Hazard Distance
MPE values adjusted by correction factors

correction_factors.name: Pulse Repetition Correction Factor
correction_factors.description: Accounts for thermal additivity in repetitive pulse trains

parameters.N: Number of pulses within T2 or time base

correction_factors.name: Extended Source Correction Factor
correction_factors.formula: C6 = α/αmin for αmin < α ≤ αmax; C6 = 1 for α ≤ αmin
correction_factors.description: Adjusts limits for extended sources based on angular subtense

correction_factors.name: Melanin Correction Factor
correction_factors.description: Accounts for reduced melanin absorption in IR-A

correction_factors.name: Extended Source MPE Factor
correction_factors.description: Converts small-source MPE to extended-source MPE

correction_factors.name: Repetitive Pulse MPE Factor
correction_factors.description: Reduces MPE for repetitive pulse exposure

measurement_parameters.name: Angular Subtense
measurement_parameters.description: Average angular dimension of apparent source

measurement_parameters.name: Maximum Angular Subtense
measurement_parameters.description: Maximum α for extended source MPE calculation

measurement_parameters.name: Angle of Acceptance
measurement_parameters.description: Field of view for radiance measurement

measurement_parameters.name: Photochemical Time Constant
measurement_parameters.description: Time at which thermal MPEs are replaced by photochemical MPEs

measurement_parameters.name: Thermal Time Constant
measurement_parameters.description: Time beyond which extended-source thermal MPEs become constant

relationships.description: IEC standard references MPE concepts from ANSI
Extended source correction applied to MPE
α compared to αmin to determine source type
α limited to αmax for certain calculations
Visible region uses T1 for thermal/photochemical transition
Extended sources use T2 for thermal limits
